report_name: Phishing Gets Smarter: Attacks Now Auto-Adapt Payloads to Victim's OS and Device

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The attack begins with a well-crafted email containing a malicious link, often with a pretext like a fake invoice or file-sharing notification.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Host Information
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use the victim's browser user-agent string, collected when the phishing link is clicked, to fingerprint the device's operating system (Windows, macOS, Android, iOS) and browser type.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Based on the victim's fingerprinted OS, the attacker's server delivers a tailored malicious payload, such as an .exe for Windows or a .dmg for macOS.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: The success of the attack relies on the user executing the dynamically delivered, OS-specific payload, such as running a downloaded executable or mounting a disk image.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: Attacker-controlled landing pages dynamically change their appearance to impersonate trusted brands like Microsoft, Zoom, or Adobe to make the phishing lure more believable.

campaigns.name: Adaptive Phishing Campaigns
campaigns.description: A modern type of phishing campaign that uses real-time device fingerprinting via user-agent strings to deliver OS-specific malicious payloads, increasing the likelihood of successful compromise.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: A trusted brand whose identity, particularly Microsoft Teams, is impersonated in adaptive phishing campaigns to make lures more convincing.

identities.name: DocuSign
identities.description: A trusted brand whose identity is impersonated in adaptive phishing campaigns to make lures more convincing.

identities.name: Zoom
identities.description: A trusted brand whose identity is impersonated on landing pages in adaptive phishing campaigns to increase believability.

identities.name: Adobe
identities.description: A trusted brand whose identity is impersonated on landing pages in adaptive phishing campaigns to increase believability.

infrastructure.name: Victim Fingerprinting Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A web landing page that analyzes a victim's browser user-agent string upon clicking a phishing link to determine their OS and device type for tailored payload delivery.

infrastructure.name: Dynamic Payload Delivery Server
infrastructure.description: Server-side infrastructure that acts as a 'traffic director', using fingerprinting data to serve a tailored, OS-specific malicious payload to the victim.

infrastructure.name: Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) Platform
infrastructure.description: Underground platforms that provide advanced, adaptive phishing kits, making sophisticated attacks like dynamic payload delivery accessible to less-skilled actors.

indicators.name: OS-Specific Payload Download from Web
indicators.pattern:
title: OS-Specific Payload Download from Web
id: 2b9c1f8a-3e4d-4b1a-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the download of high-risk file types commonly used as OS-specific payloads in adaptive phishing campaigns.
references:
  - https://www.darkreading.com/application-security/phishing-campaigns-auto-adapt-victims-device-os
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1105
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|endswith:
      - '.exe'
      - '.dmg'
      - '.bat'
      - '.ps1'
      - '.zip'
      - '.iso'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate downloads of these file types from trusted or expected sources.
indicators.description: Detects downloads of various OS-specific executable or archive files, which are characteristic of adaptive phishing campaigns that tailor payloads to the victim's system.
